page: Hetzner Cloud cpx21 is an x86_64 virtual server powered by three shared AMD EPYC physical cores operating at 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 1.3 GB of RAM per core, and 80 GB of local SSD storage. The CPU features a cache hierarchy consisting of 32 KB L1d, 0.5 MB L2, and 16 MB L3 cache, with support for AVX, AES, and SHA_NI instruction set extensions. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are bundled with this instance.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core tests placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core scaling and memory performance present notable tradeoffs. Multi-core CPU benchmarks, such as stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU tests, fall into the average range, while memory bandwidth and PassMark memory scores are weak to poor, reflecting the shared CPU allocation and low memory density. Cryptographic and compression tasks, including single-threaded gzip and PassMark encryption, demonstrate average throughput, whereas database operations and static web serving throughput are relatively weak.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the cpx21 offers a budget-friendly entry point for workloads that require moderate CPU capacity without demanding high memory throughput or dedicated core performance. The low RAM-to-vCPU ratio and shared resource model make it less suitable for memory-intensive databases, heavy compilation, or high-throughput web serving. It is best suited for lightweight applications, small-scale single-threaded processing, development environments, and basic utility workloads that can tolerate shared CPU scheduling and constrained memory bandwidth.
description: Hetzner Cloud cpx21 is an x86_64 virtual server configured with three shared AMD EPYC physical cores running at 2.0 GHz, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 80 GB of local SSD storage. Built on the QEMU hypervisor, this instance features 16 MB of L3 cache and supports AVX, AES, and SHA_NI instructions. Performance benchmarks reveal strong single-core CPU execution, though multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth are weak, with memory performance scoring in the bottom tier. Lacking bundled public IPv4 addresses, this budget-friendly server is designed for resource-constrained environments. It is best suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks, development environments, and basic utility workloads rather than memory-intensive databases or high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud cpx21 is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 3 shared AMD EPYC cores, 4.0 GB RAM, and 80 GB local SSD. It offers strong single-core performance but exhibits weak memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Hetzner Cloud cpx21 is an x86_64 virtual server with 3 shared AMD EPYC cores, 4.0 GB RAM, and 80 GB SSD, optimized for budget-friendly, light workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly shared-core virtual server featuring strong single-core performance and local SSD storage for lightweight utility workloads.
bullet_points: Three shared AMD EPYC physical cores running at 2.0 GHz
4.0 GB of system memory and 80 GB of local SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU performance in benchmark testing
Constrained memory bandwidth and multi-core scaling tradeoffs
Well-suited for lightweight utility workloads and development environments
